channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Disrupt U.S. Critical Infrastructure by Targeting Internet-Exposed PLCs
item.description: Iran-affiliated cyber actors are targeting internet-facing operational technology (OT) devices across critical infrastructures in the U.S., including programmable logic controllers (PLCs), cybersecurity and intelligence agencies warned Tuesday. "These attacks have led to diminished PLC...

item.title: Pramana: Fine-Tuning Large Language Models for Epistemic Reasoning through Navya-Nyaya
item.description: arXiv:2604.04937v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models produce fluent text but struggle with systematic reasoning, often hallucinating confident but unfounded claims. When Apple researchers added irrelevant context to mathematical problems, LLM performance degraded by 65% Apple...

item.title: Operational Noncommutativity in Sequential Metacognitive Judgments
item.description: arXiv:2604.04938v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Metacognition, understood as the monitoring and regulation of one's own cognitive processes, is inherently sequential: an agent evaluates an internal state, updates it, and may then re-evaluate under modified criteria. Order effects in cognition are...

item.title: Proximity Measure of Information Object Features for Solving the Problem of Their Identification in Information Systems
item.description: arXiv:2604.04939v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The paper considers a new quantitative-qualitative proximity measure for the features of information objects, where data enters a common information resource from several sources independently. The goal is to determine the possibility of their...

item.title: ReVEL: Multi-Turn Reflective LLM-Guided Heuristic Evolution via Structured Performance Feedback
item.description: arXiv:2604.04940v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Designing effective heuristics for NP-hard combinatorial optimization problems remains a challenging and expertise-intensive task. Existing applications of large language models (LLMs) primarily rely on one-shot code synthesis, yielding brittle...

item.title: Algebraic Structure Discovery for Real World Combinatorial Optimisation Problems: A General Framework from Abstract Algebra to Quotient Space Learning
item.description: arXiv:2604.04941v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Many combinatorial optimisation problems hide algebraic structures that, once exposed, shrink the search space and improve the chance of finding the global optimal solution. We present a general framework that (i) identifies algebraic structure, (ii)...

item.title: A Theory-guided Weighted $L^2$ Loss for solving the BGK model via Physics-informed neural networks
item.description: arXiv:2604.04971v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While Physics-Informed Neural Networks offer a promising framework for solving partial differential equations, the standard $L^2$ loss formulation is fundamentally insufficient when applied to the Bhatnagar-Gross-Krook (BGK) model. Specifically,...

item.title: Territory Paint Wars: Diagnosing and Mitigating Failure Modes in Competitive Multi-Agent PPO
item.description: arXiv:2604.04983v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present Territory Paint Wars, a minimal competitive multi-agent reinforcement learning environment implemented in Unity, and use it to systematically investigate failure modes of Proximal Policy Optimisation (PPO) under self-play. A first agent...

item.title: Enhancing sample efficiency in reinforcement-learning-based flow control: replacing the critic with an adaptive reduced-order model
item.description: arXiv:2604.04986v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Model-free deep reinforcement learning (DRL) methods suffer from poor sample efficiency. To overcome this limitation, this work introduces an adaptive reduced-order-model (ROM)-based reinforcement learning framework for active flow control. In...

item.title: Cactus: Accelerating Auto-Regressive Decoding with Constrained Acceptance Speculative Sampling
item.description: arXiv:2604.04987v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Speculative sampling (SpS) has been successful in accelerating the decoding throughput of auto-regressive large language models by leveraging smaller draft models. SpS strictly enforces the generated distribution to match that of the verifier LLM....

item.title: Prune-Quantize-Distill: An Ordered Pipeline for Efficient Neural Network Compression
item.description: arXiv:2604.04988v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern deployment often requires trading accuracy for efficiency under tight CPU and memory constraints, yet common compression proxies such as parameter count or FLOPs do not reliably predict wall-clock inference time. In particular, unstructured...

item.title: TDA-RC: Task-Driven Alignment for Knowledge-Based Reasoning Chains in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.04942v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Enhancing the reasoning capability of large language models (LLMs) remains a core challenge in natural language processing. The Chain-of-Thought (CoT) paradigm dominates practical applications for its single-round efficiency, yet its reasoning chains...

item.title: The Illusion of Latent Generalization: Bi-directionality and the Reversal Curse
item.description: arXiv:2604.04943v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The reversal curse describes a failure of autoregressive language models to retrieve a fact in reverse order (e.g., training on ``$A > B$'' but failing on ``$B < A$''). Recent work shows that objectives with bidirectional supervision (e.g.,...

item.title: Inclusion-of-Thoughts: Mitigating Preference Instability via Purifying the Decision Space
item.description: arXiv:2604.04944v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multiple-choice questions (MCQs) are widely used to evaluate large language models (LLMs). However, LLMs remain vulnerable to the presence of plausible distractors. This often diverts attention toward irrelevant choices, resulting in unstable...

item.title: Phase-Associative Memory: Sequence Modeling in Complex Hilbert Space
item.description: arXiv:2604.05030v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present Phase-Associative Memory (PAM), a recurrent sequence model in which all representations are complex-valued, associations accumulate in a matrix state $S_{t}$ $\in$ $\mathbb{C}^{d \times d}$ via outer products, and retrieval operates through...

item.title: This Treatment Works, Right? Evaluating LLM Sensitivity to Patient Question Framing in Medical QA
item.description: arXiv:2604.05051v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Patients are increasingly turning to large language models (LLMs) with medical questions that are complex and difficult to articulate clearly. However, LLMs are sensitive to prompt phrasings and can be influenced by the way questions are worded....

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Disrupt US Critical Infrastructure via PLC Attacks
item.description: Federal agencies warn attackers are manipulating PLC and SCADA systems across multiple sectors, triggering operational disruptions and raising concerns over broader OT targeting. The post Iran-Linked Hackers Disrupt US Critical Infrastructure via PLC Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Huawei Teases Pura X2, Its Most Powerful Wide Foldable Yet with HarmonyOS 6
item.description: Huawei’s Pura X2 reimagines foldables as tablet-like devices, combining a new form factor with HarmonyOS 6.

item.title: Great Wall’s Haval Unveils New Flagship “Boxy” SUV on Guiyuan Platform, Name Yet to Be Decided
item.description: Great Wall’s Haval has unveiled a new flagship boxy SUV on its Guiyuan platform, with naming still open to public input.

item.title: UBTECH Partners with Honda Trading to Advance Commercialization of Humanoid Robots and Autonomous Vehicles
item.description: UBTECH and Honda Trading are partnering to scale humanoid robots and autonomous vehicles across industrial supply chains.

item.title: NIO’s 2026 ONVO L90 to Feature In-House 5nm Autonomous Driving Chip
item.description: NIO’s ONVO L90 will debut with a self-developed 5nm chip, underscoring its ambition to control the full autonomous driving stack.

item.title: Alibaba Unveils Wan2.7-Video, Expanding AI Video Generation to Full Creative Workflows
item.description: Alibaba’s Wan2.7-Video pushes AI video generation into full creative workflows, enabling scriptwriting, editing, and scene control through simple text commands.

item.title: [AINews] Anthropic @ $30B ARR, Project GlassWing and Claude Mythos Preview — first model too dangerous to release since GPT-2
item.description: Anthropic steps up the offensive vs OpenAI's upcoming IPO woes

item.title: Azure - East US 2 (Virginia): INC0155020
item.description: Apr 7, 20:08 UTCMonitoring - Current status: We've confirmed that our service has recovered following the routing of affected traffic to healthy infrastructure, and we're continuing to monitor as the health of Snowsight continues to improve. Other services are fully recovered, but some users who...

item.title: Anthropic Unveils ‘Claude Mythos’ – A Cybersecurity Breakthrough That Could Also Supercharge Attacks
item.description: New AI model drives Project Glasswing, a effort to secure critical software before advanced capabilities fall into the wrong hands. The post Anthropic Unveils ‘Claude Mythos’ – A Cybersecurity Breakthrough That Could Also Supercharge Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Extreme Harness Engineering for Token Billionaires: 1M LOC, 1B toks/day, 0% human code, 0% human review — Ryan Lopopolo, OpenAI Frontier & Symphony
item.description: We shed light on OpenAI's first Dark Factory for the first time.

item.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
item.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens...

item.title: Russian State-Linked APT28 Exploits SOHO Routers in Global DNS Hijacking Campaign
item.description: The Russia-linked threat actor known as APT28 (aka Forest Blizzard) has been linked to a new campaign that has compromised insecure MikroTik and TP-Link routers and modified their settings to turn them into malicious infrastructure under their control as part of a cyber espionage campaign...

item.title: The New Rules of Engagement: Matching Agentic Attack Speed
item.description: The cybersecurity response to AI-enabled nation-state threats cannot be incremental. It must be architectural. The post The New Rules of Engagement: Matching Agentic Attack Speed appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Analytics Delays and Errors
item.description: Apr 7, 16:35 UTCIdentified - The cause of this issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Apr 7, 15:55 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare Analytics processing is running behind. This affects timely delivery of customer data. This also affects querying data, and some users may see errors...

item.title: Trent AI Emerges From Stealth With $13 Million in Funding
item.description: The startup has created a layered security solution aiming to secure AI agents throughout their entire lifecycle. The post Trent AI Emerges From Stealth With $13 Million in Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude.ai
item.description: Apr 7, 15:59 UTCResolved - From 14:32–15:12 UTC, we saw elevated errors impacting various Claude services, including Claude.ai and Claude Code authentication. These errors have been resolved.Apr 7, 15:22 UTCMonitoring - We have applied a fix and success rates have returned to normal. We are...

item.title: Critical Flowise Vulnerability in Attacker Crosshairs
item.description: The improper validation of user-supplied JavaScript code allows attackers to execute arbitrary code and access the file system. The post Critical Flowise Vulnerability in Attacker Crosshairs appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Docker CVE-2026-34040 Lets Attackers Bypass Authorization and Gain Host Access
item.description: A high-severity security vulnerability has been disclosed in Docker Engine that could permit an attacker to bypass authorization plugins (AuthZ) under specific circumstances. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-34040 (CVSS score: 8.8), stems from an incomplete fix for...

item.title: Desalination plants in the Middle East are increasingly vulnerable
item.description: MIT Technology Review Explains: Let our writers untangle the complex, messy world of technology to help you understand what’s coming next. You can read more from the series here. As the conflict in Iran has escalated, a crucial resource is under fire: the desalination technology that supplies water...

item.title: Cloudflare Dashboard and Cloudflare API service issues
item.description: Apr 7, 14:27 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 7, 14:20 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 7, 13:39 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Apr 7, 13:34 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and...

item.title: Enabling agent-first process redesign
item.description: Unlike static, rules-based systems, AI agents can learn, adapt, and optimize processes dynamically. As they interact with data, systems, people, and other agents in real time, AI agents can execute entire workflows autonomously. But unlocking their potential requires redesigning processes around...

item.title: Cloudflare Durable Objects Availability in Western North America
item.description: Apr 7, 13:09 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 7, 12:07 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is aware of, and is investigating issues related to Durable Objects in Western North America. Further detail will be provided as more information becomes available.

item.title: Over 1,000 Exposed ComfyUI Instances Targeted in Cryptomining Botnet Campaign
item.description: An active campaign has been observed targeting internet-exposed instances running ComfyUI, a popular stable diffusion platform, to enlist them into a cryptocurrency mining and proxy botnet. "A purpose-built Python scanner continuously sweeps major cloud IP ranges for vulnerable targets,...

item.title: [Webinar] How to Close Identity Gaps in 2026 Before AI Exploits Enterprise Risk
item.description: In the rapid evolution of the 2026 threat landscape, a frustrating paradox has emerged for CISOs and security leaders: Identity programs are maturing, yet the risk is actually increasing. According to new research from the Ponemon Institute, hundreds of applications within...

item.title: The Download: AI’s impact on jobs, and data centres in space
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The one piece of data that could actually shed light on your job and AI  Within Silicon Valley’s orbit, an AI-fueled jobs...

item.title: Cloudflare Dashboard and Cloudflare API service issues
item.description: Apr 7, 12:08 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 7, 12:02 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 7, 09:51 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Apr 7, 09:21 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating...

item.title: UK exposes Russian military intelligence hijacking vulnerable routers for cyber attacks
item.description: New advisory warns cyber threat group APT28 have exploited vulnerable edge devices to support malicious operations.

item.title: APT28 exploit routers to enable DNS hijacking operations
item.description: Russian cyber actor APT28 exploit vulnerable routers to hijack DNS, enabling adversary‑in‑the‑middle attacks and theft of passwords and authentication tokens.

item.title: Cloudflare Debian Mirror Issues
item.description: Apr 7, 11:51 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 7, 11:39 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 7, 11:08 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is aware of and investigating an issue with https://cloudflaremirrors.com/debian returning errors when...

item.title: Some users may experience issues accessing the ChatGPT website on VPN
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Image Generation (Operational) Deep Research (Operational) Connectors/Apps (Operational) Login (Operational) File uploads (Operational) Search (Operational) Conversations (Operational) Compliance API (Operational)...

item.title: [AINews] Gemma 4 crosses 2 million downloads
item.description: a quiet day lets us give due respect to the enormously successful Gemma 4 launch

item.title: Sonnet 4.6 elevated rate of errors
item.description: Apr 6, 21:45 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 6, 21:26 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Rethinking Model Efficiency: Multi-Agent Inference with Large Models
item.description: Most vision-language models (VLMs) apply a large language model (LLM) as the decoder, where the response tokens are generated sequentially through autoregression. Therefore, the number of output tokens can be the bottleneck of the end-to-end latency. However, different models may require vastly...

item.title: Incompleteness of AI Safety Verification via Kolmogorov Complexity
item.description: Ensuring that artificial intelligence (AI) systems satisfy formal safety and policy constraints is a central challenge in safety-critical domains. While limitations of verification are often attributed to combinatorial complexity and model expressiveness, we show that they arise from intrinsic...
